businessSummary.oneLiner: Government and commercial AI data analytics platform leader with Gotham (defense), Foundry (enterprise), and AIP (AI platform)
businessSummary.description: Palantir builds AI data analytics platforms for government and enterprise. FY2025 revenue hit $4.48B (+56%). US commercial revenue exceeded $500M. The Pentagon adopted Maven AI as an official program of record for all US military branches. FY2026 guidance of $7.19B-$7.41B (+60-65%) projects accelerating growth.
howTheyMakeMoney: Enterprise AI platform (Foundry) - enterprise data integration
AIP (AI Platform) - generative AI integration platform
AIP Boot Camps - rapid customer onboarding and conversion

keyMetrics.name: P/E (TTM)
keyMetrics.interpretation: ~10x the S&P 500 average (23-25x) and ~6x the software sector average (35-40x). Extreme premium, but FY2026E Forward P/E of 114x compresses rapidly on 65% growth

keyMetrics.name: Forward P/E
keyMetrics.interpretation: Based on FY2026E EPS of $1.34. Still expensive but PEG of 2.48 is within high-growth stock range given 65% revenue growth

keyMetrics.name: Revenue Growth (YoY)
keyMetrics.interpretation: FY2025 revenue $4.48B. AI demand explosion drove 56% YoY growth. FY2026 guidance at 65%

keyMetrics.name: Operating Margin
keyMetrics.interpretation: Top-tier profitability among software companies. Combined with 82% gross margin, demonstrates strong earnings power

keyMetrics.name: FCF Margin
keyMetrics.interpretation: FCF of $2.10B. Exceptional cash generation with minimal debt ($229M)

keyMetrics.name: Cash Position
keyMetrics.interpretation: Net cash $6.95B ($2.90/share). Near-zero leverage financial health

keyMetrics.name: Revenue Backlog
keyMetrics.interpretation: Multi-year revenue visibility. Approximately 2.5x current annual revenue

growthDrivers.title: Maven AI Officially Adopted by All US Military
growthDrivers.description: Deputy Secretary of Defense memo on March 9 designated Maven Smart System as official program of record. Long-term deployment across all military branches confirmed, securing stable defense revenue

growthDrivers.title: Iran Conflict Real-World AI Deployment
growthDrivers.description: Per NYT, Palantir operates a system analyzing Pentagon intelligence using Anthropic AI technology. Combat validation provides powerful evidence for additional contracts

growthDrivers.title: FY2026 Revenue Guidance $7.19B-$7.41B (+60-65%)
growthDrivers.description: Management projecting growth acceleration. US commercial segment emerging as key engine, reducing government dependency

growthDrivers.title: Navy ShipOS $448M + Army $10B Contracts
growthDrivers.description: Major military contracts secured consecutively. $11.2B backlog provides long-term revenue visibility

growthDrivers.title: Morgan Stanley Raises Target to $205, Increasing Bullishness
growthDrivers.description: Morgan Stanley's Sanjit Singh set $205 target maintaining Hold. Not bullish enough to upgrade, but notably more optimistic. UBS $200 (Strong Buy) and Wedbush $230 also maintained

growthDrivers.title: AIP Boot Camp Accelerating Commercial Conversion
growthDrivers.description: Short-term trial programs rapidly acquiring enterprise customers. US commercial revenue surpassed $500M

competitiveAdvantage.summary: 20+ years of government defense AI track record and top-level security clearances, Maven official military adoption, NVIDIA sovereign AI partnership. Iran conflict combat deployment provides unmatched validation

moats.description: Ontology platform deeply integrated into government/military systems. Replacement costs are prohibitively high once deployed
Top-level security clearances and 20+ years of government trust. Virtually impossible for new entrants to match
AIP Boot Camps drive rapid customer acquisition while Ontology data model expands ecosystem reach
Real-time target identification in Iran conflict. Combat data is the ultimate competitive advantage

competitors.name: Anduril Industries
competitors.detail: Defense AI/drone startup. Hardware-focused, more complementary than competitive with Palantir's software platform

competitors.name: Snowflake / Databricks
competitors.detail: Enterprise data platform competitors. Significantly behind Palantir in government/defense access

competitors.name: Microsoft / AWS
competitors.detail: Cloud giants entering government AI. Scale advantage but struggle to match Palantir's deep Ontology integration

competitors.name: C3.ai
competitors.detail: Enterprise AI platform. Trails Palantir significantly in both revenue scale and growth rate

recentNews.headline: Morgan Stanley raises target to $205, maintains Hold with increased optimism
recentNews.significance: Analyst Sanjit Singh not bullish enough to upgrade but notably more positive. Revenue acceleration and Maven adoption cited as key factors

recentNews.headline: Pentagon adopts Maven AI as core US military system
recentNews.significance: Deputy SecDef memo designates Maven Smart System as program of record. Long-term military-wide deployment confirmed, stabilizing defense revenue

recentNews.headline: Silicon Valley defense bets pay off as Palantir AI deployed in Iran conflict
recentNews.significance: NYT reports Anthropic AI + Palantir system analyzing Pentagon intelligence. Real-world AI combat validation

recentNews.headline: UBS raises target from $180 to $200, Strong Buy
recentNews.significance: Analyst Karl Keirstead upgrades target by 11% citing revenue acceleration and Maven adoption

recentNews.headline: $11.2B revenue backlog, $10B Army contract, 61% growth forecast
recentNews.significance: FY2026 guidance at 61% growth. Major military contracts and backlog secure long-term visibility

analystOpinions.notableComment: Morgan Stanley's Sanjit Singh: $205 target, Hold maintained but notably more bullish. UBS Karl Keirstead: $200 Strong Buy. Wedbush Dan Ives: $230 maintained. Rosenblatt also raised to $200

risks.title: Extreme Valuation
risks.description: TTM P/E of 239x is 10x the S&P 500 average. Even Morgan Stanley cited valuation as the reason for withholding an upgrade. Any growth disappointment could trigger sharp declines

risks.title: War-Dependent Revenue Risk
risks.description: Iran conflict deployment boosted defense revenue, but conflict resolution could weaken growth momentum. War-driven growth raises sustainability questions

risks.title: Share Dilution
risks.description: Share count increased 4.67% YoY. Stock-based compensation continuously dilutes existing shareholder value

risks.title: Government Budget Volatility
risks.description: US government shutdowns or defense budget cuts would directly impact revenue. Government revenue share remains significant

risks.title: Ethics/Reputation Risk
risks.description: Growing ethical criticism of AI weapons targeting systems. Swiss magazine lawsuit and press freedom concerns add pressure

risks.title: Competition
risks.description: Microsoft, AWS entering government AI market. Limited short-term threat but warrants long-term monitoring

buyReasons.title: Dominant Position in Defense AI
buyReasons.rationale: Maven officially adopted by US military. 20+ years of government trust and security clearances are priceless assets

buyReasons.title: Growth Accelerating, Not Decelerating
buyReasons.rationale: FY2025 +56% to FY2026 +65% guidance. Revenue growth is speeding up, not slowing down

buyReasons.title: Explosive Commercial Segment Growth
buyReasons.rationale: US commercial revenue exceeded $500M. AIP Boot Camps rapidly expanding enterprise base, healthily reducing government dependency

buyReasons.title: Overwhelming Financial Strength
buyReasons.rationale: Cash $7.18B, debt $229M, FCF margin 47%. Strong earnings, minimal debt, excess cash

buyReasons.title: Wall Street Target Upgrades Trending
buyReasons.rationale: Consensus $196 (+30% upside). Morgan Stanley $205, UBS $200, Wedbush $230. Major firms consistently raising targets

overallOpinion: Maven AI officially adopted as the US military's core system. Combined with Iran conflict deployment, Palantir has cemented its unmatched position in defense AI
Morgan Stanley increased optimism with a $205 target, joining UBS ($200) and Wedbush ($230) in the bull camp. $500M commercial milestone and $11.2B backlog add growth visibility
However, P/E of 239x is so extreme that even Morgan Stanley withheld an upgrade because of it. Growth expectations leave zero margin for error
Below both 50-day and 200-day MAs with RSI at 51 neutral. Short-term correction phase, but fundamental momentum is undeniable

investmentVerdict.summary: Maven's military-wide adoption, Iran combat validation, and Morgan Stanley's increased bullishness confirm best-in-class fundamentals. FY2026 +65% guidance and commercial acceleration set a clear direction. However, P/E 239x demands discipline: dollar-cost averaging on pullbacks is the prudent approach.

sources.name: TheStreet
sources.description: Morgan Stanley $205 target, Hold maintained with increased optimism

sources.name: StockAnalysis
sources.description: PLTR price, valuation, and financial metrics

sources.name: Reuters via Investing.com
sources.description: Pentagon Maven AI official adoption exclusive

sources.name: New York Times
sources.description: Silicon Valley defense AI investments, Palantir Iran deployment

sources.name: Motley Fool
sources.description: $11.2B revenue backlog, $10B Army contract, 61% growth forecast

sources.name: Motley Fool
sources.description: PLTR valuation problem yet buy case analysis

x.hook: Military adoption + Morgan Stanley bullish, but P/E 239x
x.text:
$PLTR (Mar 23) Maven AI officially adopted by US military. Morgan Stanley raised target to $205 with increased optimism. But P/E at 239x. $150.68.

1/4
thread:
Commercial revenue hit $500M, backlog $11.2B, FY2026 +65% guidance. UBS $200, Wedbush $230. Wall Street keeps raising targets.

2/4

The catch: P/E 239x. Even Morgan Stanley wouldn't upgrade because of it. One earnings miss and the stock gets crushed.

3/4

Below 50/200-day MAs, RSI 51 neutral. Consensus $196 (+30.4%). Best fundamentals in AI defense, but valuation demands discipline. DCA on pullbacks.

4/4

threads.hook: Military adoption + Morgan Stanley bullish, but P/E 239x
threads.text:
$PLTR (Mar 23) $150.68 (-3.21%)

Maven AI officially adopted by all US military branches. Morgan Stanley raised target to $205 with increased optimism. UBS $200, Wedbush $230.

Commercial $500M milestone, $11.2B backlog, FY2026 +65% guidance. But P/E 239x made even Morgan Stanley hold off on an upgrade.

Consensus $196 (+30.4%). Best fundamentals in defense AI, valuation is the only concern. DCA on dips.

witty.hook: Wins wars, can't win Wall Street
witty.text:
$PLTR (Mar 23) Proven in actual combat. US military officially adopted it. Morgan Stanley raised target. Stock? $150.68, down 3.21%. Wins wars but P/E 239x is hard to win over.

1/4
thread:
Commercial $500M, backlog $11.2B. Analysts say buy. Morgan Stanley says 'great but expensive.' The diplomacy of Wall Street.

2/4

P/E 239x. 10x the S&P 500 average. Everyone knows it's expensive. Everyone also knows there's no alternative.

3/4

RSI 51, neutral. Consensus $196 (+30.4%). Is this expensive or cheap for a defense AI monopoly? Depends on your time horizon.

4/4

empathy.hook: Great news, but Palantir holders still worry
empathy.text:
$PLTR (Mar 23) Maven adopted by military, Morgan Stanley more bullish. Good news, but $150.68 (-3.21%). If you're holding, the mixed feelings are real.

1/4
thread:
Commercial $500M, backlog $11.2B, +65% growth. Fundamentals have never been stronger. The worry is that the stock already priced it in.

2/4

P/E 239x creates anxiety. Morgan Stanley couldn't upgrade because of it. That tension between great company and expensive stock is exhausting.

3/4

Consensus $196 (+30.4%). If you believe in the long-term story, pullbacks are opportunities. DCA to manage the risk.

4/4

Maven adopted, Morgan Stanley more bullish. Best news ever, stock drops. If you're holding, the frustration is real.
